name: The Far Side — Radio Blackout Zone
description: The spacecraft passes behind the Moon and all communication with Earth ceases for 45 minutes. In this silence, the crew is more alone than any human beings have ever been.
atmosphericDetails: The radio goes silent mid-sentence; Houston's last transmission cuts off as though a door has been closed
The Moon fills the window — grey and ancient and completely indifferent, close enough to see individual craters
In the silence, the only sounds are the crew's breathing, the soft hiss of the environmental system, and the occasional click of a circuit breaker
The Earth is not visible from this position; the crew is on the far side of a world from every other human being alive
Lovell looks at the lunar surface passing below and thinks about the Fra Mauro Highlands — the place he was supposed to walk — and says nothing
